title: MD
biography: Cynthia Maung is a Burmese physician who founded the Mae Tao Clinic on the Thailand-Myanmar border in 1989, providing healthcare to refugees, migrants, and displaced persons. The clinic now serves over 100,000 patients annually and has become one of the most important healthcare providers for displaced populations in Southeast Asia.
aiSummary: Cynthia Maung is a primary care and refugee health specialist at the Mae Tao Clinic, Thailand-Myanmar border. Founded clinic serving 100,000+ displaced patients annually. Based in Mae Sot, Thailand.

timeline.title: Fled Myanmar after military crackdown
timeline.description: Left Myanmar following the violent suppression of the pro-democracy uprising

timeline.title: Founded Mae Tao Clinic
timeline.description: Established the clinic with minimal supplies in a small building on the Thai-Myanmar border

timeline.title: Ramon Magsaysay Award
timeline.description: Received Asia's highest honor for community leadership

timeline.title: Clinic serving 100,000+ patients per year
timeline.description: Mae Tao Clinic expanded to provide comprehensive healthcare including surgery, maternal care, and training
